chunks.title: NBA Advanced Metrics Primer
chunks.heading: True Shooting Percentage
chunks.content:
True Shooting Percentage，常写成 TS%，用来衡量球员得分效率。它比普通命中率更全面，因为它同时把两分球、三分球和罚球都考虑进来了。一个球员如果三分和罚球都很强，TS% 往往会比只看投篮命中率更能反映他的真实效率。

TS% 高，通常意味着球员每次出手能换回更多分数。但它并不直接等于进攻能力强于一切，因为它没有单独表示持球压力、失误代价、战术牵制和关键球难度。真正分析球员时，TS% 最适合与使用率、助攻失误比和回合占有情况一起看。

chunks.title: NBA Advanced Metrics Primer
chunks.heading: Effective Field Goal Percentage
chunks.content:
eFG% 是对普通投篮命中率的修正，因为三分球的价值高于两分球。它会给命中的三分球更高权重，从而更准确地描述投篮结构带来的收益。一个大量命中三分的球员，eFG% 可能会显著高于传统命中率。

eFG% 特别适合比较两名不同出手结构的球员。例如，一个中距离占比很高的球员，即便命中率不低，也可能因为缺少三分和罚球而在 eFG% 上落后于空间型后卫。

chunks.title: NBA Advanced Metrics Primer
chunks.heading: Usage Rate
chunks.content:
Usage Rate 通常用来描述球员在场时，有多少回合以他的出手、失误或制造罚球结束。它不是触球率，也不是持球时间。一个球员可以经常持球组织，但如果他把回合更多地分配给队友，Usage Rate 不一定很高。

高使用率往往意味着更大的战术责任。球队把更多决定权交给这个球员，他需要处理更多强防守回合、关键时间球和临时应变。高使用率与高效率同时存在时，通常说明球员具备很强的进攻核心价值。

chunks.title: NBA Advanced Metrics Primer
chunks.heading: Offensive and Defensive Rating
chunks.content:
Offensive Rating 通常描述球员或球队每一百回合大致能得到多少分，Defensive Rating 则描述每一百回合大致会让对手得到多少分。它们本质上是节奏修正后的效率指标，适合跨球队、跨阵容、跨节奏环境做比较。

不过，单看个人防守效率时要特别小心，因为它会强烈受队友、轮换体系和对位策略影响。一个球员所在阵容的防守沟通很好，他的个人 Defensive Rating 可能就会比实际单防能力显得更漂亮。

chunks.title: Reading NBA Box Scores with Context
chunks.heading: Raw Totals vs Per Possession Thinking
chunks.content:
看 NBA 数据时，最常见的误区之一是把原始总数直接当作全部结论。得分、篮板、助攻当然重要，但如果不结合出场时间、回合数和比赛角色，很容易误判。例如，同样是二十五分，一个球员可能用了大量出手才拿到，另一个球员则可能在较少球权下更高效完成。

因此，箱分数据更适合做第一层观察，而不是最后结论。真正解释一个球员为什么表现好，通常要把出手结构、失误、罚球、对位环境和比赛时间段一起放进来看。

chunks.title: Reading NBA Box Scores with Context
chunks.heading: Plus Minus and Lineup Noise
chunks.content:
正负值是很好用的提示信号，但不是孤立裁决。它能够告诉我们球员在场时球队净胜还是净负，可是它也会受到队友、对手轮换、垃圾时间和单场波动影响。一个正负值很高的球员，不一定意味着他单独主宰比赛；一个正负值很差的球员，也不一定说明他就是失利主因。

更稳妥的做法，是把正负值当作线索，然后继续看录像、阵容搭配、回合过程和比赛上下文。这样才能避免只看一个数字就下最终判断的问题。

chunks.title: NBA League Evolution Overview
chunks.heading: From Interior Game to Spacing Era
chunks.content:
NBA 的战术演化，大体经历了由内线主导、强对抗半场阵地战，逐渐走向空间化、提速化和三分化的过程。早期很多冠军球队都依赖中锋或低位核心，通过篮下终结、篮板控制和半场纪律建立优势。随着外线投射、挡拆组织和规则环境的变化，比赛越来越重视空间创造与回合效率。

现代 NBA 常见的五外、四外一内、换防体系、快速推进和大量三分出手，并不是突然出现的，而是长期规则、数据分析和球员能力进化共同推动的结果。三分球的重要性提升以后，球队更强调在同样回合数里拿到更高的理论收益。

chunks.title: NBA League Evolution Overview
chunks.heading: Superstars and Team Building
chunks.content:
NBA 历史里，超级球星始终是争冠的中心资源。无论是传统内线支柱、持球大核，还是攻防一体的锋线，冠军球队往往需要至少一位可以改变防守策略的顶级球员。围绕球星建队时，管理层通常会考虑三件事：球权如何分配、空间如何保证、防守如何托底。

在不同年代，争冠拼图的形态不一样。以前的强队常常依赖双塔、低位和长轮换；现代球队则更重视外线投射、换防能力、无球协作和板凳的功能性。虽然形式变化很大，但让核心球员的优点被最大化、弱点被最小化这条原则始终没变。

chunks.title: NBA League Evolution Overview
chunks.heading: Why History Matters for Q&A
chunks.content:
做 NBA 知识问答时，历史并不只是讲故事。它能帮助解释为什么某些数据在不同年代不能直接横比，为什么同样的得分数字在不同节奏环境里价值不同，也能帮助说明某些球员或球队的影响力并不只体现在基础数据上。

如果用户问为什么某位球员在那个年代这样打，答案往往既要看个人能力，也要看当时的规则、空间环境、对抗尺度和联盟平均战术水平。历史背景越清楚，解释就越不容易流于表面。

chunks.title: NBA Player Archetypes and Evaluation
chunks.heading: Ball Dominant Creators
chunks.content:
持球核心通常承担最高难度的进攻任务，包括挡拆阅读、错位惩罚、末节决策和关键球处理。评价这类球员时，不能只看得分总量，还要看失误控制、包夹出球、对防守阵型的牵制力，以及在高强度比赛里是否还能维持效率。

一个真正的顶级持球核心，往往能在对手明确知道他要发起进攻的情况下，依旧稳定创造高质量回合。这样的球员会显著改变队友的出手质量，也会让教练在战术布置上拥有更高容错。

chunks.title: NBA Player Archetypes and Evaluation
chunks.heading: Two-Way Wings
chunks.content:
攻防一体的锋线是现代 NBA 最抢手的类型之一，因为他们可以在不占用过多球权的同时，兼顾持球补充、外线投射、换防和协防覆盖。季后赛强度上来以后，这类球员的价值通常进一步放大，因为他们在不同阵容间切换时更容易保持稳定。

评价两翼球员时，常见误区是只盯着单场得分。实际上，他们的真正价值经常体现在让阵容更容易成立，例如承担弱侧协防、帮核心顶最难的对位、在不持球时完成高效率终结。

chunks.title: NBA Player Archetypes and Evaluation
chunks.heading: Big Men in Modern Systems
chunks.content:
现代内线不再只是背身和抢篮板。高水平中锋往往要同时具备至少一项精英价值，比如护框、顺下终结、策应、外拆投射或短顺下处理球。内线如果既不能保护篮筐，也不能在进攻端提供明确功能，就很容易在高端比赛里被针对。

因此评价内线时，需要先看他到底为阵容提供了什么确定性。是防守端让对手不敢轻易杀进来，还是进攻端能作为掩护后处理球枢纽，或者能为外线创造额外空间。不同类型的内线，适配的球队核心也会完全不同。

chunks.title: NBA Core Rules and Game Flow
chunks.heading: Game Structure
chunks.content:
NBA 正式比赛由四节组成，每节十二分钟。常规时间结束后如果比分相同，会进入五分钟加时赛，直到分出胜负。比赛节奏之所以比很多业余联赛更快，一方面是四节制和暂停管理更精细，另一方面是进攻时间、回场、发球和换人规则都强调连续性。

NBA 的主教练和球员管理比赛的方式，通常会围绕回合价值展开。因为一场比赛的回合数有限，所以每一次失误、每一次无效犯规、每一次低质量出手都会放大影响。理解 NBA 规则时，最好不要把它只看成“裁判怎么吹”，而要同时理解这些规则为什么会塑造现代比赛的节奏。

chunks.title: NBA Core Rules and Game Flow
chunks.heading: Shot Clock and Backcourt
chunks.content:
NBA 进攻方通常拥有二十四秒完成一次有效进攻。有效进攻不是单纯把球投出去，而是要在时间结束前出手，并且篮球触及篮筐。如果球出手后碰到篮筐并且进攻方继续控制球，通常会获得新的进攻时间；不同回合情境下，重置后的时长并不完全相同。

后场推进也有严格限制。球队在后场获得控球后，必须在规定时间内把球推进到前场。如果球已经合法进入前场，再把球主动带回后场，会构成回场违例。这个规则的核心目的，是防止球队拖慢节奏、无限消耗时间。

chunks.title: NBA Core Rules and Game Flow
chunks.heading: Violations and Fouls
chunks.content:
违例和犯规的区别很重要。违例通常指的是非法动作或时间、位置类错误，例如走步、两次运球、脚踢球、三秒违例、回场违例。犯规则是对对手身体接触、非法防守或比赛秩序的破坏。违例一般导致交换球权，犯规则可能导致边线发球、罚球，或者累计到全队犯规数后带来额外处罚。

防守三秒是 NBA 比较有代表性的规则。它要求防守球员不能长时间站在禁区内而又不积极防守对位对象。这个规则的存在，是为了给突破和持球进攻创造空间，避免禁区里长期站着协防中锋破坏观赏性。

chunks.title: NBA Core Rules and Game Flow
chunks.heading: Goaltending and Basket Interference
chunks.content:
干扰球和篮筐干扰是很多新球迷容易混淆的概念。简单理解，干扰球更多发生在球的飞行阶段，当球已经在下降轨迹并且有机会入筐时，防守方不能非法触碰。篮筐干扰则更强调对篮筐、篮板和篮网周围空间的非法接触，尤其是在球仍然有可能通过篮筐圆柱体时。

这些规则的目标，是保护一次合法投篮的自然结果。如果没有这些限制，球员可以在球接近篮筐时直接把球拨走，投篮命中率和战术价值都会被破坏。

Section III—Replay Review Process

- Except as set forth in Rule 14 below, replay reviews will be triggered by the on-court game officials and Replay Center Official in accordance with the rules set forth in Section I above. The Replay Center Official will determine the final outcome on all replays triggered pursuant to Section I above except Flagrant Fouls and Altercations, which will be decided by the on-court officials, with personnel in the Replay Center playing a supporting role. In all cases, the two game officials will inform both team

For replay reviews to be determined by the Replay Center Official, the calling official, and the crew chief at his/her discretion, will go to the scorer’s table and put on the headset to receive the ruling from the Replay Center Official and the other on-court official(s) will prepare the players to return to play. If the ruling is to be made by the on-court officials, the crew chief and a second official will go to the scorer’s table and make the ruling with the assistance of the Replay Center Official.

a. A team’s basket consists of the basket ring and net through which its players try to shoot the ball. The visiting team has the choice of baskets for the first half. The basket selected by the visiting team when it first enters onto the court shall be its basket for the first half. b. The teams change baskets for the second half. All overtime periods are considered extensions of the second half. c. Five sides of the backboard (front, two sides, bottom and top) are considered in play when contacted by the basketball. A dribble is movement of the ball, caused by a player in control, who throws or taps the ball to the floor.

a. The dribble ends when the dribbler:

- For a player who is in control of the ball while dribbling, the gather is defined as the point where a player does any one of the following:(1) Puts two hands on the ball, or otherwise permits the ball to come to rest, while he is in control of it;

Section V—Free Throw

A free throw is the privilege given a player to score one point by an unhindered attempt for the goal from a position directly behind the free throw line. This attempt must be made within 10 seconds.

Section VI—Frontcourt/Backcourt

a. A team’s frontcourt consists of that part of the court between its endline and the nearer edge of the midcourt line, including the basket and inbounds part of the backboard. b. A team’s backcourt consists of the entire midcourt line and the rest of the court to include the opponent’s basket and inbounds part of the backboard. c. A ball being held by a player: (1) is in the frontcourt if neither the ball nor the player is touching the backcourt, (2) is in the backcourt if either the ball or player is touching the backcourt. d. A ball being dribbled is (1) in the frontcourt when the ball and both feet of the player are in the frontcourt, (2) in the backcourt if the ball or either foot of the player is in the backcourt. e. The ball is considered in the frontcourt once it has broken the plane of the midcourt line and is not in player control. f. The team on offense must bring the ball across the midcourt line within 8 seconds.

Frontcourt/backcourt status is not attained until a player with the ball has established a positive position in either half during (1) a jump ball, (2) a steal by a defensive player, (3) a throw-in in the last two minutes of the fourth period and last two minutes of any overtime period or (4) any time the ball is loose.

Section VII—Held Ball

A held ball occurs when two opponents have one or both hands firmly on the ball or when a defensive player touches the ball causing the offensive player to return to the floor with the ball in his continuous possession which would result in a traveling violation.

A held ball should not be called until both players have hands so firmly on the ball that neither can gain sole possession without undue roughness. If a player is lying or sitting on the floor while in possession, he should have an opportunity to throw the ball.

chunks.title: NBA Rulebook Definitions

a. A pivot takes place when a player, who is holding the ball, steps once or more than once in any direction with the same foot, with the other foot (pivot foot) in contact with the floor. b. If the player wishes to dribble after a pivot, the ball must be out of his hand before the pivot foot is raised off the floor. If the player raises his pivot off the floor, he must pass or attempt a field goal before the foot is returned to the floor.

If he fails to follow these guidelines, he has committed a traveling violation.

Section IX—Traveling

Traveling is progressing in any direction while in possession of the ball, which is in excess of prescribed limits as noted in Rule 4—Section VII and Rule 10—Section XIII.

A screen is the legal action of a player who, without causing undue contact, delays or prevents an opponent from reaching a desired position.

Section XI—Field Goal Attempt

Section XI—Field Goal Attempt

A field goal attempt is a player’s attempt to shoot the ball into his basket for a field goal. The act of shooting starts when, in the official’s judgment, the player has started his shooting motion and continues until the shooting motion ceases and he returns to a normal floor position. For jump shots, the shooting motion starts when the offensive player starts to bring the ball upward towards the basket. On drives to the basket or other moving shots, the shooting motion starts when the player gathers the ball and continues through with a sho

chunks.title: NBA Rulebook Definitions
chunks.heading: Overview
chunks.content: shots, the shooting motion starts when the player gathers the ball and continues through with a shot (except during a take foul situation when the clocks are not expiring, the shooting begins when the player’s shoulders start upward). It is not essential that the ball leave the shooter’s hand. His arm(s) might be held so that he cannot actually make an attempt.

chunks.title: NBA Rulebook Definitions
chunks.heading: Overview
chunks.content:
ball leave the shooter’s hand. His arm(s) might be held so that he cannot actually make an attempt.

The term is also used to include the flight of the ball until it becomes dead or is touched by a player. A tap during a jump ball or rebound is not considered a field goal attempt. However, anytime a live ball is in flight toward the rim from the playing court, the goal, if made, shall count, even if time expires or the official’s whistle sounds. The field goal will not be scored if time on the game clock expires before the ball leaves the player’s hand or the ball is in flight toward the rim.

Section XII—Throw-In

chunks.title: NBA Rulebook Definitions
chunks.heading: Overview

re the ball leaves the player’s hand or the ball is in flight toward the rim.

Section XII—Throw-In

A throw-in is a method of putting the ball in play from out-of-bounds in accordance with Rule 8—Section III. The throw-in begins when the ball is given to and controlled by the player inbounding, or at his disposal, and ends when the ball is released.

Section XIII—Last Two Minutes

When the game clock shows 2:00, the period is considered to be in the two-minute period.

Section XIV—Suspension of Play

hows 2:00, the period is considered to be in the two-minute period.

Section XIV—Suspension of Play

An official can suspend play for retrieving an errant ball, re-setting the timing devices, delay-of-game warning, inadvertent whistle, instant replay, a seriously-injured player or any other unusual circumstance. During such a suspension, neither team is permitted to substitute and the defensive team may not be granted a timeout. Play shall be resumed at the point of interruption.

chunks.title: NBA Rulebook Definitions
chunks.heading: Overview
chunks.content:
he defensive team may not be granted a timeout. Play shall be resumed at the point of interruption.

EXCEPTIONS: (1) Suspension of play for a player bleeding. See Comments on the Rules—N. (2) Seriously-injured player. Player must be removed and opponent is permitted one substitution.

Section XV—Point of Interruption

The Point of Interruption is where the ball is located when the whistle sounds.

Section XVI—Team Control

A team is in control when a player is holding, dribbling or passing the ball. Team control ends when the defensive team deflects the ball or there is a field goal attempt.

Section XVII—Team Possession

Section XVII—Team Possession

A team is in possession when a player is holding, dribbling or passing the ball. Team possession ends when the defensive team gains possession or the ball hits the rim of the offensive team.

Section XVIII—Fumble

A player who is holding the ball and fumbles it out of his control may recover the ball. If his pivot foot moves to recover the ball, he must then pass or shoot the ball. If he fumbles and recovers it without moving his pivot foot and before the ball touches the floor, he retains his status before the fumble.

chunks.title: NBA Team Building and Roles
chunks.heading: Rotation Logic
chunks.content:
一支 NBA 球队的轮换并不是简单地把最强五个人一直放在场上。教练需要在持球点、护框、换防、投射、篮板和第二阵容火力之间做平衡。很多常规赛强队，会用比较稳定的轮换节奏确保长时间的战术执行；而到了季后赛，轮换通常会缩短，把更多时间集中到高价值阵容上。

判断一支球队的风格时，可以先看它如何处理三个问题：谁来发起进攻，谁来承担弱侧终结，谁来做防守端的最后一道保险。不同球队即使阵容名气接近，也会因为这三项配置不同而呈现出完全不同的比赛样貌。

chunks.title: NBA Team Building and Roles
chunks.heading: Offensive Roles
chunks.content:
现代 NBA 的常见进攻角色包括持球核心、二次发起点、终结型内线、定点射手、空切连接者和替补持球手。好的球队不会让所有人都做同一种事，而是把球员放在最容易稳定发挥的职责上。角色分工越清晰，球队在高压比赛中越不容易因为犹豫而停滞。

例如，一个高使用率核心身边通常需要能拉开空间的射手和愿意掩护顺下的内线；如果身边全是需要球权才能发挥的球员，整体效率往往会下滑。阵容搭配的核心，不是堆叠名气，而是减少功能重叠。

chunks.title: NBA Team Building and Roles
chunks.heading: Defensive Roles
chunks.content:
防守端的分工同样重要。护框中锋、机动锋线、外线压迫者和沟通型协防者，经常共同决定一支球队的防守上限。很多球迷会把防守简单理解成单防能力，但 NBA 真正高水平的防守更依赖整体站位、协防时机和轮转纪律。

如果一个球队缺少明确的防守支点，它在季后赛很容易被反复点名；如果它拥有优秀的防守中轴，即便进攻偶尔波动，也能维持下限。这也是为什么争冠球队通常都会非常重视禁区保护和侧翼尺寸。
